items.headline: Gold prices today, Tuesday, June 16: Prices firming ahead of Fed meeting
items.summary: Gold August futures opened at $4,331.30 per troy ounce on Tuesday, down 0.5% from Monday's closing price. The price of gold edged higher this morning, shifting to $4,365.80 per troy ounce as of 6:49 a.m. ET.

items.headline: Bond traders drastically shift Fed rate-hike bets
items.summary: Bond traders took a beat on their interest-rate hike forecasts as the White House signaled a peace agreement was underway to end the U.S.-Iran war. items.headline: The Bond Market Is Lukewarm on the Iran Deal. What It’s Seeing That Stocks Aren’t.
items.summary: Pessimism comes naturally to the bond market, largely because many of the dynamics that power other markets higher ultimately erode its value.  Commodity price hikes do the same, only more rapidly, while stock market rallies offer higher-yielding alternatives to their staid relations in the fixed-income market.  Equity markets are charging higher on the back of a reported deal to end the U.S.-Iran war—even though an officially agreed-upon text has yet to be signed by either side—just as the Federal Reserve prepares to meet for the first time under new Chairman Kevin Warsh.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Traders Reduce Bets on a Fed Rate Rise This Year
items.summary: News of a deal between Washington and Tehran that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz has prompted investors to pare back bets on higher rates this morning.  The thinking: If oil starts flowing more freely, energy prices would likely fall, reducing inflationary pressure and giving the Fed less reason to increase rates.  It's a reversal from just a week ago, when investors were still digesting news of a stronger-than-expected May jobs report.
items.why_it_matters: News appears more supportive of sentiment or growth expectations.
